invariants: Generated booking commands remain shadow/non-executable in registry discovery.
The demo mutation must be reversible without cancel/delete/payment/destructive flow.
Organization/session/user authority must come from trusted host runtime context.
Phase 1 binding does not encode future adapter status; adapter mount posture belongs to the next runtime-adapter slice.

selectedRead.why: Availability is computed from schedule rules and is the safest app-aware proof that the agent understands the current booking context before attempting a workflow mutation.

selectedMutation.why: A hold is a real booking/reservation inventory workflow mutation, but it is temporary, has TTL, performs no payment/deposit behavior, and can be released as cleanup.

seedFixture.resourceTargetRule: If the host page context or env exposes resourceUnitId or resourceCombinationId, pass it through createHold. If neither is available, proceed only when the selected context is explicitly context-capacity based and createHold accepts a context-only hold; otherwise fail before mutation with missing-resource-target.

rejectedForV02Demo.reason: Creates a durable booking record; cleanup would require cancel semantics, which the ralplan excluded from the demo path.
Cancel-like workflow is explicitly out of scope for v0.2.
Commits a temporary hold into a durable booking; defer until cancellation/rollback semantics are explicitly approved.
